content.full_text:
March 2, 1992


Richard H. McManus
486 Murray Drive
El Cajon, CA  92020

                                        Re:  Your Request for Assistance
                                             Our File No. G-92-090

Dear Mr. McManus:

     This is in response to your letter to the Fair Political
Practices Commission concerning the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").  While we appreciate your concerns and interest in the
problems and issues in your community, the issues you have raised
in your letter do not appear to be within the jurisdiction of the
Fair Political Practices Commission.

     While the Commission possesses broad powers to interpret and
implement the provisions of the Act, including the power to adopt
regulations to carry out the Act's purposes, the Commission's
jurisdiction is limited to the provisions of the Act.  (Section
83112.)  If you believe there has been a violation of the
provisions of the Act, you may wish to file a complaint with our
Enforcement Division.  They may be reached at (916) 322-6441.

     If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        John W. Wallace
                                        Counsel, Legal Division

JWW:aa
Enclosures


1     The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code
Sections 81000-91015, with regulations appearing at 2 California
Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq.  (See generally, The
Political Reform Act of 1974 as Amended to January 1, 1991, copy
enclosed.)  We have also enclosed some other general information
on the Political Reform Act for your information.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

sections.conclusion: The issues raised do not appear to be within the jurisdiction of the Fair Political Practices Commission, as the Commission's jurisdiction is limited to provisions of the Political Reform Act.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor raised concerns about problems and issues in their community that they believed fell under FPPC jurisdiction.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined jurisdiction, finding that the requestor's concerns do not fall within the Commission's authority under the Political Reform Act. The FPPC directed the requestor to file a complaint with the Enforcement Division if there is a violation of the Act's provisions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; document is a declination letter. Question and conclusion content inferred from body text. File number G-92-9-9 referenced.

QUESTION: The requestor raised concerns about problems and issues in their community that they believed fell under FPPC jurisdiction.

CONCLUSION: The issues raised do not appear to be within the jurisdiction of the Fair Political Practices Commission, as the Commission's jurisdiction is limited to provisions of the Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responded to Richard H. McManus's letter regarding community concerns, declining jurisdiction and explaining that the Commission's authority is limited to the Political Reform Act. The letter directed the requestor to contact the Enforcement Division if a violation of the Act occurred.
